feature_name: PCIe Root Complex Gen4 x1/x2/x4/x8 Interface
description: Implements a PCI Express Root Complex supporting up to Gen4 (16.0 GT/s) operation. Lane width is configurable to x1, x2, x4, or x8. Provides full link training via the LTSSM with status visibility. Supports downstream device enumeration via ECAM. Equalization is available for robust high-speed operation.
evidence: Spec §5 Feature Summary (PCIe), §9.1 PCI Express, §8.2 PCIE_LINK_CTRL/STATUS
dependencies: Requires PD_PCIE powered, REFCLK_PCIE at 100 MHz SSC-compliant, GBL_CTRL.CLK_EN=1, and PCIE_LINK_CTRL.LTSSM_EN with TARGET_SPEED/NUM_LANES configured

description: Provides an option to synthesize the controller with Gen5 capability for higher bandwidth. When enabled, link training negotiates up to the Gen5 target speed. Software can cap speed using TARGET_SPEED. Equalization controls remain applicable. Actual availability depends on PHY support and synthesis configuration.
dependencies: Requires Gen5-capable PHY, proper board design, and PCIE_LINK_CTRL.TARGET_SPEED set to Gen5 when available

description: Supports logical bifurcation of an x8 link into two x4 downstream ports. This enables connecting multiple devices while maintaining aggregate bandwidth. Configuration is platform/strap dependent. Software discovery reflects two logical ports. Link training proceeds independently per x4 group.
dependencies: Requires PHY and board routing support for bifurcation and appropriate strap or configuration settings

description: Provides ≥8 outbound and ≥8 inbound translation regions. Maps SoC physical address ranges to PCIe address space for MMIO and configuration accesses. Inbound regions whitelist BAR hits into SoC memory. Region control includes base, limit, target, and type. Enables isolation and flexible memory mapping.
evidence: Spec §5 Feature Summary (PCIe), §8.2 IATU_* registers, Appendix B
dependencies: Requires programming of IATU_OB_* and IATU_IB_* registers before use; depends on AXI and PCIe clocks active

feature_name: PCIe ECAM Window for Downstream Config Space
description: Exposes an ECAM aperture to access downstream devices' configuration space. Software can scan bus/device/function using standard PCIe enumeration flows. Base address is programmable in ECAM_BASE_LO/HI. ECAM can be placed within a translated outbound region. Ensures standards-compliant config cycles.
dependencies: Requires iATU outbound CFG region configured and ECAM base programmed; PD_PCIE and SoC interconnect clocks must be on

description: Generates and handles MSI signaling for downstream devices and internal functions. Global MSI enable and steering registers are provided. MSI interrupts can be routed to the SoC GIC via the aggregator. Supports allocation and masking via standard PCIe capabilities. Facilitates scalable interrupt delivery without legacy pins.
dependencies: Requires MSI address/data programming and interrupt aggregator routing; coherent memory not required for MSI

description: Supports MSI-X with programmable vector table and Pending Bit Array (PBA) in system memory. Allows many interrupt vectors per function for fine-grained steering. Works with the interrupt aggregator for CPU affinity. Enables masking at vector granularity. Improves performance for multi-queue devices.
dependencies: Requires MSI-X table/PBA located in accessible memory and routing configured in aggregator; coherent memory recommended

description: Implements AER to detect, log, and report correctable and uncorrectable errors. Provides sticky status and first error header capture. Software can enable, mask, and clear events. Enhances diagnosability and reliability on complex fabrics. Integrates with global error summary.
dependencies: Requires AER enable in AER_CTRL and appropriate interrupt routing; PD_PCIE must be on

description: Supports Active State Power Management L0s and L1 to reduce link power during idle. Policy is software-controlled based on latency budgets. Works with downstream devices that negotiate ASPM. Can be enabled after link stabilizes. Provides dynamic power savings without full link down.
dependencies: Requires link up and PM_ASPM_CTRL configured; platform must meet latency requirements

description: Supports deep low-power link substates L1.1 and L1.2 for further power reduction. Entry is negotiated per PCIe spec with capable devices. Software enables after validating latency tolerance. Wakes on activity or PME. Complements ASPM L1 for aggressive savings.
dependencies: Requires device support, correct PM policy, and stable REFCLK; may need wake sources configured

description: Handles PCIe PME signaling to wake the subsystem from low power. Aggregator routes PME to the SoC GIC. Enables remote wake from downstream devices. Status is visible for software action. Integrates with domain power control for resume flows.
dependencies: Requires PD_PCIE retention or wake logic active and aggregator routing; clocks must resume for handling

description: Provides per-lane transmitter presets and receiver hints. Supports automatic equalization and software-assisted tuning. Registers expose detailed control for link optimization. Improves signal integrity over longer or challenging channels. Status registers aid validation and tuning.
dependencies: Requires link training cycles and access to EQ registers; PHY must support presets

description: Supports issuing and handling hot reset to retrain or recover links. Software can trigger via port logic or secondary bus reset semantics. LTSSM transitions to Detect and retrains automatically. Used for recovery and re-enumeration. Works without full chip reset.
dependencies: Requires PD_PCIE on and downstream device tolerance; software must manage re-enumeration

description: Optionally supports ARI for extended function addressing. Enables more functions per device and efficient routing. Beneficial for virtualization and multifunction devices. Discovery via PCIe capabilities. Software should enable when endpoints support it.
dependencies: Requires ARI-capable endpoints and proper capability negotiation

description: Optionally supports ACS to enforce isolation and prevent inappropriate peer-to-peer. Controls request redirection, completion, and upstream forwarding. Enhances security in multi-device topologies. Advertised via capabilities. Works with IOMMU for strong isolation.
dependencies: Requires ACS-capable hierarchy and software enablement; benefits increase with IOMMU present

description: Optionally implements Address Translation Services, Page Request Interface, and Process Address Space IDs. Enables device-side caching of translations and per-process isolation. Improves performance for shared-memory accelerators. Requires OS and IOMMU support. Exposed via PCIe capabilities.
dependencies: Requires system IOMMU, OS drivers, and endpoint support; configure PASID limits and ATS policy

feature_name: PCIe SR-IOV (up to 64 VFs per PF)
description: Supports Single Root I/O Virtualization with up to 64 Virtual Functions per Physical Function. Enables efficient sharing of devices among guests. Managed via SR-IOV capability structures. Works with MSI-X for per-VF interrupts. Requires platform virtualization stack.
dependencies: Requires SR-IOV capable endpoints/functions, OS hypervisor support, and sufficient MSI-X vectors

description: Optionally supports PCIe IDE for link-layer integrity and confidentiality. Protects TLPs against tampering and eavesdropping. Requires key provisioning and policy. Interoperates only with IDE-capable devices. Useful in hostile physical environments.
dependencies: Requires IDE-capable endpoints, key management, and PHY support; may affect latency

description: Optionally supports FLIT mode to increase efficiency with fixed-size flow control units. Reduces overhead and improves throughput at high speeds. Requires compatible link partners. Mode selection is negotiated or configured. Intended for advanced PHY/link layers.
dependencies: Requires PHY/link support and endpoint compatibility; software must select mode if configurable

description: Emulates legacy INTx signaling on the root port when required. Allows compatibility with devices expecting INTx. Aggregator maps events to SoC interrupts. Coexists with MSI/MSI-X. Useful during early boot or legacy OS scenarios.
dependencies: Requires routing in interrupt aggregator and device configuration; may need ECAM accesses

description: Integrates a single-port SuperSpeed USB controller capable of dual-role operation. Default mode is Host per specification. Supports root hub functions and standard port management. Registers follow a simplified XHCI-like model. Backward compatible to HS/FS/LS via companion functionality.
evidence: Spec §5 Feature Summary (USB), §9.2 USB, §8.4 USB Host
dependencies: Requires PD_USB power, USB PHY clocks (125/60 MHz), and port power sequencing; pin mux as platform-specific

description: Operates with USB 2.0 signaling rates for wide device compatibility. Manages link states and transitions across speeds. Handles split transactions where applicable. Ensures enumeration and transfer reliability across legacy devices. Complements SuperSpeed host capabilities.
dependencies: Requires appropriate PHY and companion path; port power and VBUS control must be active

feature_name: USB Port Power, Overcurrent, and Wake-on-Connect
description: Provides per-port power control to manage VBUS. Monitors and reports overcurrent conditions. Supports wake on device connect for low-power resume. Events are surfaced via USBSTS/INTR. Enables robust and power-aware USB operation.
dependencies: Requires PD_USB retention/wake logic; INTR_ENABLE configured; external power switch or PHY support for OC detection

feature_name: I2C: 4 Master Controllers up to 1 MHz (FM+)
description: Includes four independent I2C master instances. Supports Standard, Fast, and Fast-mode Plus speeds up to 1 MHz. Works with 7-bit and 10-bit addressing. Provides transmit and receive FIFOs. Interrupts and status allow polled or event-driven operation.
dependencies: Requires PD_PERIPH power, I2C clock dividers configured, and pull-ups on SDA/SCL lines

description: Supports multi-master arbitration per I2C specification. Allows coexistence with other masters on the bus. Clock stretching by slaves is tolerated. Software should manage bus contention. Enhances flexibility in complex systems.
dependencies: Requires correct bus pull-ups and timing; SPEED and RESTART_EN configured as needed

description: Honors slave-driven clock stretching on SCL. Prevents overrun in slower peripherals. Integrated into controller timing logic. Visible via status when stretching occurs. Ensures protocol compliance with diverse devices.
dependencies: Requires timing registers configured for selected speed and stable peripheral clocks

feature_name: SPI: 2 Masters up to 50 MHz, Modes 0–3, up to 8 CS
description: Provides two independent SPI master controllers. Supports up to 50 MHz SCK with modes 0, 1, 2, and 3. Each instance can control up to eight chip selects. Transmit and receive paths are full duplex. Interrupts report status and errors.
dependencies: Requires PD_PERIPH power, SPI_BAUD configured, and chip select lines wired via pinmux

feature_name: SPI Programmable Word Size (up to 32 bits)
description: Allows framing of transfers with up to 32-bit word sizes. Programmable via control registers for protocol compatibility. Simplifies integration with wide-register peripherals. Reduces CPU overhead by packing data. Works with all SPI modes.
dependencies: Requires SPI_CTRL0.FRAME_SIZE configured and endpoints supporting selected word width

description: Optionally connects SPI data movement to the DMA engine. Enables high-throughput, low-CPU transfers. Uses channel handshakes or memory-based descriptors. Improves latency consistency under load. Beneficial for large or continuous streams.
dependencies: Requires DMA channel allocation and SPI/DMA handshake configuration; PD_DMA and PD_PERIPH on

feature_name: UART: 2x 16550-Compatible up to 4 Mbps with FIFOs and RTS/CTS
description: Integrates two UARTs compatible with 16550 programming model. Supports baud rates up to 4 Mbps. Includes 16-byte or larger FIFOs to reduce interrupts. Optional hardware flow control with RTS/CTS. Provides standard interrupt and status registers.
dependencies: Requires PD_PERIPH power, baud divisors configured (IBRD/FBRD), and pins muxed for UART/RTS/CTS

feature_name: GPIO: 32 Lines with Interrupt-on-Change, Debounce, Drive/Pull
description: Exposes 32 configurable GPIOs. Each pin supports direction, pull, and drive strength control. Debounce filters spurious edges. Interrupts can be edge or level with polarity control. Status and clear registers support rapid ISR handling.
dependencies: Requires PD_PERIPH power and pinmux assignment; INT routing via aggregator must be configured

description: Provides general-purpose timers for scheduling and a watchdog for system recovery. Timers support load, start/stop, and interrupt on expiry. Watchdog includes control, clear, and lock registers. Enables periodic tasks and fail-safe resets. Integrated into interrupt framework for event handling.
dependencies: Requires PD_PERIPH power and clock; interrupt routing configured; watchdog lock sequence observed

feature_name: DMA Engine: 8 Channels, Ring and Linked-List Modes
description: Integrates an 8-channel DMA engine for memory-to-device and device-to-memory transfers. Supports ring mode with descriptor rings and linked-list mode for flexible queues. Doorbells advance producer indices for asynchronous enqueue. Per-channel interrupts signal descriptor and transfer completion. Global configuration controls QoS and burst behavior.
evidence: Spec §5 Feature Summary (DMA), §6 Block Architecture (DMA), §8.3 DMA_*
dependencies: Requires PD_DMA power, DMA_GBL_CFG.DMA_EN=1, channel descriptors in accessible memory, and interrupt routing

description: DMA channels can source or sink to PCIe MMIO or memory via iATU mappings. Enables high-throughput data movement with minimal CPU. Works for device register streaming or host memory accesses. Coherency attributes are programmable for system integration. Complements MSI/MSI-X for completion signaling.
evidence: Spec §5 Feature Summary (DMA), §9.7 DMA, Appendix B
dependencies: Requires iATU regions programmed, DMA channels configured, and appropriate AXI attributes; ensure ordering as needed

feature_name: DMA QoS and AXI Burst Length Programmability
description: Allows software to tune AXI QoS for arbitration friendliness. Burst length controls balance latency and efficiency. Facilitates coexistence with CPU and GPU traffic. Useful for meeting real-time constraints. Configured via DMA_GBL_CFG.
dependencies: Requires PD_DMA active and interconnect support for QoS/burst hints

feature_name: AXI4 Master and Inbound AXI4 Slave Windows
description: Data plane uses AXI4 master for DMA and PCIe outbound traffic. An AXI4 slave window accepts inbound PCIe traffic to memory-mapped targets. Ensures high bandwidth and standard integration. Supports coherency attributes where applicable. Provides scalability across SoCs.
dependencies: Requires AXI fabric connectivity and clocking; coherent interconnect if coherency used

feature_name: Interrupt Aggregator: 128 Inputs to GIC with Programmable Routing
description: Collects interrupts from PCIe, DMA, USB, I2C, SPI, UART, GPIO, and timers. Provides enable/disable, masking, and status per source. Routes up to eight outputs to SoC GIC with CPU affinity control. Simplifies interrupt management and scaling. Supports MSI/MSI-X steering.
dependencies: Requires aggregator ROUTE_CFG programmed and GIC configuration; per-source enables must be set

description: Provides software-initiated resets at global and per-domain levels. GBL_CTRL.SOFT_RESET offers top-level reset. Power domain control includes per-block resets. Facilitates clean reinitialization without power cycling. Coordinates with PCIe hot reset for link-specific recovery.
dependencies: Requires clocks active to take effect; software must reprogram context after reset

description: Implements separate power domains for fine-grained gating. Software can power on/off interfaces to save energy. Domains retain state when configured for retention. Power transitions are visible in status. Enables aggressive power management policies.
dependencies: Requires coordination with clock gating and quiesce of in-flight transfers; wake sources configured for resume

description: Supports multiple low-power states beyond simple clock gating. Retention preserves critical state for fast resume. OFF achieves maximal savings with power gating. Entry/exit sequences are provided for safe transitions. Wake events re-enable power and clocks.
dependencies: Requires software-controlled sequences via GBL_CTRL and PWR_DOMAIN_CTRL; wake sources must remain powered

feature_name: Configurable Wake Sources (PCIe PME, USB Connect, GPIO, Timer)
description: Allows specific events to wake the subsystem from low power. PCIe PME, USB connect, GPIO edge, and timer expiry are supported. Aggregator conveys wake cause to software. Helps implement low-power idle strategies. Minimizes latency to resume.
dependencies: Requires retention of wake logic and proper interrupt routing; device-side PME/wake must be enabled

feature_name: Clocking: REFCLK_PCIE 100 MHz, AXI/APB 50–200 MHz, USB PHY 125/60 MHz
description: Defines required clocks for stable operation of subsystems. PCIe requires a 100 MHz SSC-compliant reference. AXI/APB control clocks run from 50–200 MHz. USB PHY clocks of 125/60 MHz are used as needed. Per-block dividers are available under software control.
dependencies: Requires board-level clock sources or PLLs, GBL_CTRL.CLK_EN asserted, and clock domain sequencing

feature_name: Sticky Error/Status and Global Interrupt Summary
description: Provides sticky status registers to capture error and event history. Global interrupt summary aids quick ISR entry decisions. Software clears bits after logging. Improves post-mortem analysis. Reduces need for intrusive tracing.
evidence: Spec §8.1 GBL_STATUS/GBL_INT_STATUS, §14 Error Handling and Debug
dependencies: Requires periodic polling or ISR handling to harvest and clear sticky bits

description: Includes optional performance counters for throughput and latency. Helps profile workloads and tune settings. Counters can be read without disturbing data paths. Facilitates regression testing and capacity planning. Availability may vary by synthesis.
dependencies: Requires debug blocks enabled in configuration and clocks active during measurement

description: Optionally supports JTAG boundary scan for production test. Enables pin-level verification and board bring-up. Non-intrusive access to IO cells is provided. Complements software diagnostics. Useful in manufacturing and field service.
dependencies: Requires JTAG pins connected and test access enabled per platform policies

description: DMA and PCIe AXI master transactions can be tagged with security attributes. Supports separation of secure and non-secure traffic. Privilege levels can be enforced by the interconnect. Aids in building trusted execution environments. Configured via DMA global registers.
dependencies: Requires SoC interconnect honoring attributes and correct OS/firmware configuration

feature_name: Inbound Access Whitelisting via iATU Filters
description: Inbound iATU regions restrict PCIe BAR hits to approved SoC ranges. Prevents unauthorized memory access. Regions are disabled by default until programmed. Software controls base/limit to enforce policy. Works alongside IOMMU for defense in depth.
dependencies: Requires programming of inbound regions and disabling unused windows; optional IOMMU enhances protection

feature_name: Integration with SoC IOMMU for PCIe Inbound Traffic
description: Supports use with a system IOMMU to translate and isolate inbound PCIe traffic. Enables per-device or per-process mappings. Reduces risk from malicious or buggy devices. Improves virtualization safety. Recommended for complex deployments.
dependencies: Requires IOMMU hardware and OS drivers; configure ATS/PRI/PASID if leveraging device-side translation

feature_name: CPU Affinity and Vector Routing in Interrupt Aggregator
description: Routes interrupt sources to specific GIC SPI lines and CPUs. Enables balancing load across cores. Supports masking and prioritization per source. Improves real-time responsiveness. Simplifies integration with SMP OS schedulers.
dependencies: Requires ROUTE_CFG programming and GIC target configuration; per-source enables must be set

feature_name: Programmable Coherency Attribute on DMA Transactions
description: Allows marking DMA accesses as coherent or non-coherent per system policy. Ensures correct cache behavior and data visibility. Reduces software cache maintenance when coherent. Set through DMA global configuration. Harmonizes with interconnect coherence domains.
dependencies: Requires coherent interconnect or ACE/CHI bridges when set; OS must map buffers appropriately

feature_name: Per-Lane Equalization Presets Exposed via Registers
description: Exposes detailed equalization controls on a per-lane basis. Software can read current status and adjust presets. Assists compliance and margining tests. Helps isolate SI issues during bring-up. Complements automatic equalization.
dependencies: Requires link training cycles and per-lane register access; PHY support for presets is needed

description: Provides live link state including up/down, current speed, negotiated lanes, and training status. Aids diagnostics and recovery flows. Enables policy decisions for power management. Useful during enumeration and performance tuning. Reduces reliance on external analyzers.
dependencies: Requires PD_PCIE on and LTSSM activity; software must poll or handle interrupts for updates

description: Adopts an XHCI-inspired register model for familiarity. Simplifies porting of standard USB host stacks. Includes run/stop and reset controls. Provides event and port status registers. Streamlines software integration.
dependencies: Requires driver adaptation to vendor subset and proper initialization sequence

description: Peripheral blocks provide status and error bits that clear on read. Simplifies ISR servicing with minimal writes. Prevents stale status from lingering. Harmonizes error handling across blocks. Improves robustness under high interrupt rates.
evidence: Spec §14 Error Handling and Debug, peripheral register descriptions
dependencies: Requires ISR design to read status before other operations; ensure ordering when multiple flags exist

description: Watchdog includes a lock mechanism to prevent accidental reconfiguration. Ensures integrity of safety-critical timeouts. Requires explicit unlock sequence for updates. Helps meet safety goals in embedded systems. Reduces risk from errant software.
dependencies: Requires correct lock/unlock sequence and privileged access controls in software
